entry.title: [JR] The Ultimate Home Theater Chairs? | Valencia Tuscany Ultimate Heat and Ventilation Review
entry.summary:
I upgraded my home theater seating to the Valencia Home Theater Tuscany Ultimate Heat and Ventilation — and I’m breaking down everything you need to know before you buy.
In this video I cover the build quality, the reclining mechanism, how they actually feel during a long movie session, and whether the price is justified for a serious home theater setup.
Shop Valencia Seating: https://us.valenciatheaterseating.com/?sca_ref=450738.9jpx0B96w0
If you’re searching for the best home theater chairs, home theater recliners, or movie room seating, Valencia is one of the most recommended brands at this price point — and the Tuscany Ultimate with Heating and Ventilation is their flagship.
Whether you’re building a dedicated home theater from scratch or upgrading an existing setup, choosing the right seating makes or breaks the experience. These chairs are designed for long viewing sessions, with features I’ll cover like power recline, USB charging, cupholders, storage, and saved seating presets.

entry.title: [JR] Disney Just Killed Home Entertainment | What This Means for Physical Media
entry.summary:
Disney just laid off its entire home entertainment team. Yes, the entire team responsible for Blu-rays, 4Ks, and physical media is gone.
So what does this actually mean? Is Disney stepping away from physical media completely? And what does this mean for collectors going forward?
In this video, I break down what’s happening, why this move matters, and what it could mean for the future of physical media as a whole.

entry.title: [JR] Movie Collecting Doesn’t Feel The Same Anymore…
entry.summary:
For a long time, movie collecting was about the hunt. Digging through shelves. Stumbling on a hidden gem. Finding something rare for a great price when you least expected it.
That feeling is mostly gone.
Today, collecting feels like endless online shopping. Limited editions announced months in advance. Preorders, countdown timers, and “out of stock” notices within minutes. Scalpers and resellers are flipping everything before fans even have a chance.
In this video, I talk about how physical media collecting has changed, why the thrill of the hunt has disappeared, and how the hobby has shifted from discovery to mindless cart-filling. What we gained, what we lost, and whether there’s any way to get that magic back.
If you’ve ever missed the feeling of finding something special on a shelf, this one’s for you.

entry.title: [JR] Mini-LED vs OLED in 2026 | Sony Bravia 9 4K TV Review
entry.summary:
In this video, I review the Sony Bravia 9 Mini-LED 4K TV after a couple months of real world use in my home theater. Sony positions the Bravia 9 as its flagship Mini-LED television, aimed at viewers who want OLED-level performance without the typical OLED trade-offs. So are they right?
I break down how the Bravia 9 performs with movies, TV shows, gaming, and everyday viewing, focusing on brightness, contrast, local dimming, motion handling, and upscaling. I also compare Mini-LED vs OLED where it actually matters, not just on a spec sheet.
This is not a first-impressions video. It’s a practical look at whether the Sony Bravia 9 is worth buying if you’re shopping for a high-end 4K TV in 2026.
Topics covered: • Mini-LED vs OLED real-world performance • Brightness, contrast, and blooming control • 4K HDR performance (Blu-ray movies and streaming) • Who this TV is for and who should skip it

entry.title: [JR] What Netflix Buying Warner Brothers Means for Physical Media
entry.summary:
Netflix winning the bid to buy Warner Brothers is a massive moment for Hollywood, but the biggest question for collectors is simple. What happens to physical media if this deal goes through?
Warner’s home entertainment division is currently part of the SDS joint venture with Universal, and that partnership controls everything from 4K discs to Blu rays to GRUV. If Netflix takes over WB, that entire ecosystem could change overnight. Netflix has never cared about discs. They’ve avoided physical media entirely unless a director forces it. So does this deal put the future of Warner’s catalog at risk? Could boutique labels like Criterion, Arrow, Kino Lorber, Vinegar Syndrome, and others end up becoming the real home for these movies? Or does SDS survive the corporate shakeup?
In this video I break down what this acquisition could mean for collectors, theatrical windows, boutique labels, and the long term outlook for owning movies in a world where streaming giants want everything behind a subscription.
If you care about 4K, Blu ray, steelbooks, boutique labels, or the future of film preservation, this is the video you want to watch.

entry.title: [JR] This App Has Better Movies Than Most Paid Services
entry.summary:
Kanopy might be the best free streaming option that way too many movie fans overlook. In this video, I walk through how to sign up, what you need to access it, and why its catalog quietly rivals the paid services.
You only need a library card, and once you are in you get ad free streaming, true cinema focused curation, and a library of classic films, award winners, indie gems, international releases, documentaries, and more. This is not another free catalog full of filler. This is the good stuff.
Head to Kanopy,com to check if your library or university participates and sign-up today!

entry.title: [JR] The 5 Movies That Turned Me Into Who I Am Today
entry.summary:
In this video, I share five movies that say more about me than any bio ever could. These films shaped my taste, the way I think about storytelling, and why I love cinema as much as I do. If you have seen them, you probably get me already. If not, consider this a watchlist that explains my personality through film.
This is not a ranking or a list of the greatest movies ever made. Just a personal look at the films that left fingerprints on who I am as a movie fan and a person.
Let me know what five movies explain you in the comments. I am always curious what films shape other people!

entry.title: [JR] Chris Stuckmann Made a Movie. Let’s Talk About Shelby Oaks
entry.summary:
Chris Stuckmann releasing Shelby Oak is a big deal, not just for him as a filmmaker, but for creators who grew up on this platform and are pushing beyond it.
This video is part discussion about Shelby Oaks and part conversation about what happens when content creators move into new creative worlds. I talk about the film, the reactions to it, the challenge of reviewing work from someone who comes from the same space, and why some people will never accept YouTubers who step outside the platform.
I also reflect on my own journey as a creator, writing my book, and thinking about long term creative goals beyond YouTube. Building a community here is powerful, but it can also be the launchpad to bigger creative ambitions. Watching Chris take this leap feels like a natural evolution of the creator space, and it is exciting to see.

entry.title: [JR] The Best Horror Movie Sequels Ever Made
entry.summary:
Horror sequels usually crash and burn…but not these. These are the rare few that matched the original, or even outdid it.
Not all horror sequels are created equal — but some are just as legendary as the originals. In this video, I’m breaking down the best horror movie sequels of all time, from classic slashers to modern masterpieces that prove great horror doesn’t stop after the first film.
We’ll talk about Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter, A Nightmare on Elm Street 3: Dream Warriors, The Conjuring 2, Doctor Sleep, and The Exorcist III — five sequels that became essential horror viewing.
These films show how horror can evolve, surprise, and even surpass the original. Whether it’s the brutal energy of Friday the 13th Part 4, the creativity of Dream Warriors, or the haunting atmosphere of The Conjuring 2 and Doctor Sleep, these movies prove that horror sequels can be just as powerful as the original, and sometimes, even better.
If you’re a horror fan, collector, or just love revisiting classic franchises, this list is for you.

entry.title: [JR] Master and Commander 4K UHD Blu-ray Review | Is the Audio Really That Bad?
entry.summary:
In this review, I break down the newly released Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World on 4K Ultra HD Blu-ray. From the jaw-dropping visuals and Dolby Atmos sound to the disc’s special features, packaging, and transfer quality, find out if this is a must-own for your physical media collection. Plus, what's up with that bass? We will dive in!
Directed by Peter Weir and starring Russell Crowe and Paul Bettany, Master and Commander is a modern naval epic that deserves a high-end presentation. But does this 4K Blu-ray deliver?
Whether you’re a collector or just upgrading from DVD or Blu-ray, this review will help you decide if Master and Commander deserves a spot on your shelf.
